2604.13713.title: Learning the Cue or Learning the Word? Analyzing Generalization in Metaphor Detection for Verbs
2604.13713.paper_summary: Metaphor detection models achieve strong benchmark performance, yet it remains unclear whether this reflects transferable generalization or lexical memorization. To address this, we analyze generalization in metaphor detection through RoBERTa, the shared backbone of many state-of-the-art systems, focusing on English verbs using the VU Amsterdam Metaphor Corpus. We introduce a controlled lexical hold-out setup where all instances of selected target lemmas are strictly excluded from fine-tuning, and compare predictions on these Held-out lemmas against Exposed lemmas (verbs seen during fine-tuning). While the model performs best on Exposed lemmas, it maintains robust performance on Held-out lemmas. Further analysis reveals that sentence context alone is sufficient to match full-model performance on Held-out lemmas, whereas static verb-level embeddings are not. Together, these results suggest that generalization is primarily driven by "learning the cue" (transferable contextual patterns), while "learning the word" (verb-specific memorization) provides an additive boost when lexical exposure is available.
2604.13713.paper_summary_zh: 隱喻檢測模型在基準測試中表現優異，但尚不清楚這是否反映了可轉移的泛化或詞彙記憶。為了解決這個問題，我們通過 RoBERTa 分析隱喻檢測中的泛化，該模型是許多最先進系統的共享骨幹，專注於使用 VU 阿姆斯特丹隱喻語料庫的英語動詞。我們引入了一種受控的詞彙保留設置，所有選定目標詞根的實例都嚴格排除在微調之外，並將這些保留詞根的預測與曝光詞根（在微調期間見過的動詞）進行比較。儘管模型在曝光詞根上的表現最佳，但在保留詞根上仍然保持穩健的性能。進一步的分析顯示，僅僅句子上下文就足以匹配保留詞根的完整模型性能，而靜態動詞級嵌入則無法實現。綜合這些結果表明，泛化主要是由「學習提示」（可轉移的上下文模式）驅動，而「學習單詞」（動詞特定的記憶）在詞彙曝光可用時提供了額外的提升。
2604.13713.author: Sinan Kurtyigit et.al.
2604.13713.authors: Sinan Kurtyigit, Sabine Schulte im Walde, Alexander Fraser
